title: Hymenophyllum latifolium
intro: Hymenophyllum latifolium, also known as the Broad-leaved Rupturewort, is a type of fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, providing various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The bioactive constituents of Hymenophyllum latifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from respiratory problems to skin conditions.
